title: Scaphyglottis amparoana
taxonomy: Scaphyglottis amparoana belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This genus is part of the subtribe Cranichidinae within the tribe Cranichideae. The subtribe Cranichidinae is characterized by its unique flowers and growth habits. Further classification places Scaphyglottis amparoana within the tribe Cranichideae of the subfamily Orchidoideae. The species is native to Central and South America.

intro: Scaphyglottis amparoana, also known as the orchid Scaphyglottis amparoana, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. Some of the bioactive constituents of Scaphyglottis amparoana include flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, and phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid. Herbal preparations made from Scaphyglottis amparoana, such as teas and infusions, are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin problems.
